title: The Brothers Karamazov
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
content: “If everything is permitted, then everything is hateful.”
action: Context: The realization that a system without boundaries or core values eventually collapses into internal nihilism.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer
content: “Of all the creatures that breathe and move upon the earth, nothing is bred that is weaker than man.”
action: Context: The baseline acknowledgment of human fragility when set against the scale of natural and divine forces.

title: Waiting for Godot
author: Samuel Beckett
content: “They give birth astride of a grave, the light gleams an instant, then it's night once more.”
action: Context: The stark observation of the temporal limitation of human activity.

title: Inferno
author: Dante Alighieri
content: “The hottest places in hell are reserved for those who in time of moral crisis preserve their neutrality.”
action: Context: The assertion that inaction is, itself, a decisive and consequential choice.

title: The Stranger
author: Albert Camus
content: “I opened myself to the gentle indifference of the world.”
action: Context: The moment an individual aligns their internal reality with the objective neutrality of the external environment.
